claims.summary: Phase cards and witness queues should make transitions replyable without forcing action.
claims.disposition: Verified current language-only cards, witness states, right-to-ignore delivery budget, and no-control authority.

claims.summary: Capacity or readiness to transition is not the same as ease of movement through viscosity.
claims.disposition: Preserved as a substantive felt distinction and verified current schema keeps capability, gate, friction, viscosity, and processing speed orthogonal.

claims.summary: A stable or habitable structural label can coexist with functional stalling and exertion.
claims.disposition: Preserved as unscored felt evidence; no telemetry or card state is allowed to negate it.

claims.summary: Held phase-card count should be compared with density gradient and pressure without assuming one causes the other.
claims.disposition: Routed to a bounded read-only phase-card and telemetry replay.

claims.summary: The phase system should represent capacity and viscosity as separate parameters.
claims.disposition: Verified transition_capabilities_v1, transition_gate_v1, viscosity_index, subjective friction, friction window, and processing-speed modifier provide the requested separation.
